title: Pet Urine on Carpet
timeEstimate: 30 min – 24 hr enzyme dwell; pad contamination = pro
stainChemistry: Pet urine = urea + uric acid + pheromones + protein. Uric acid CRYSTALLIZES on drying — bonds tightly to carpet fiber. Crystals reactivate with humidity, bacteria metabolize them, smell returns weeks after "cleaning." Cat urine adds felinine (sulfur-amino-acid) → bacterial breakdown → mercaptans (the sharp persistent cat-pee smell). The CHEMISTRY UNLOCK on synthetic carpet (nylon / polyester / olefin) is enzyme cleaner — uric acid digests enzymatically. NO other common chemistry does this. CRITICAL FORK: WOOL CARPET = different card (pet-urine-wool-carpet). Retail pet enzymes contain protease which DIGESTS WOOL. On synthetic carpet, retail pet enzymes are safe and effective.
whyThisWorks: Enzyme cleaner digests uric acid crystals — required step. Saturation matters more than product: enzyme has to reach wherever urine reached. Cover with damp towel + plastic wrap to keep enzyme wet during dwell. Cold water, Dawn before enzyme to clear surface fats. Aggressive weighted extraction prevents pad wick-back. The hard ceiling: pad contamination = pro sub-surface extraction (truck-mount NOT enough — water pressure injection needed). 2 home rounds max.
defaultAssumption: Synthetic carpet (nylon / polyester / olefin) — most home wall-to-wall. WOOL carpet = pet-urine-wool-carpet card (different protocol, pro lane). Cat urine harder than dog (felinine + mercaptans). Recurring same-spot accidents = pad almost certainly contaminated, sub-surface extraction.

spottingProtocol.instruction: UV blacklight in dark — urine glows yellow-green. Tape outer edge with painter's tape. Pet urine spreads farther than visible — treating too small means the dog re-marks.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen cloth with cold water, press on spot, lift, rotate. Cold dilutes uric acid + prevents protein setting. Weighted dry towel 5 min. Never pour.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn damp cloth before enzyme step. Lifts surface fat / salts so enzyme reaches uric acid below. NO OxiClean — alkaline can set protein.

spottingProtocol.agent: Pet Enzyme Cleaner — Saturate to Pad Depth
spottingProtocol.equipment: Pet-specific enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution); damp towel + plastic wrap; tape
spottingProtocol.instruction: On SYNTHETIC carpet only. Apply 2–3× the urine volume — enzyme reaches uric-acid depth. Cover with damp towel + plastic wrap, tape edges. Fresh: 30–60 min. Overnight accident: 24 hr.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: WOOL carpet = different card (retail pet enzymes contain protease which digests wool). Enzymes deactivated by heat / chlorine / strong alkali — don't layer.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Uncover. Weighted paper towels overnight. Fan-dry.
Nose to pile + blacklight in dark. Still positive = ONE more enzyme round + 24-hr dwell. Still positive after 2 rounds = pad contamination, sub-surface extraction.

steps: 1. Blacklight scan + tape full contaminated area.
3. Dawn damp cloth — clear surface for enzyme.
4. Pet enzyme — saturate to pad depth, cover, dwell 30 min – 24 hr.
6. Re-blacklight + smell. 2 rounds max. Positive = sub-surface extraction.
7. WOOL CARPET: separate card (pet-urine-wool-carpet) — protease digests wool.
products: Pet enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution) — synthetic carpet only
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — heat sets protein + deactivates enzyme
warnings: WOOL CARPET = different card (protease digests wool)
NEVER hot water / steam — sets protein + deactivates enzymes
NEVER OxiClean / alkaline before enzyme — can set protein
Over-wetting carries urine into pad — saturate enzyme, not water
2 home rounds max — pad contamination = pro sub-surface extraction (NOT truck-mount)

homeSolutions.instruction: Before anything: is this wool carpet? Wool carpet has its own protocol (pet-urine-wool-carpet) because retail pet enzyme cleaners (Nature's Miracle, Rocco & Roxie, Anti Icky Poo, Simple Solution) all contain protease — protease digests wool just like it digests urine protein. If your carpet is wool, stop and use the pet-urine-wool-carpet card. This card is for SYNTHETIC carpet (nylon, polyester, olefin).

homeSolutions.agent: UV Blacklight — Find the Whole Spot
homeSolutions.instruction: Pet urine almost always spreads farther than the visible spot. Get a cheap UV blacklight off Amazon (~$15). In a dark room, sweep it across the area — urine glows pale yellow-green. Mark the edges with painter's tape. Without a blacklight, treat at least 4 inches beyond the visible edge. Treating too small = the dog re-marks the same spot.

homeSolutions.agent: The Two Rules — No Heat, No Ammonia
homeSolutions.instruction: (1) NO hot water / steam / dryer — heat permanently sets the protein, kills uric acid enzymes, AND bakes the odor in. (2) NO ammonia-based cleaners — they smell like urine to your pet and invite re-marking. Skip vinegar+baking soda Pinterest tricks — they cancel each other and don't touch uric acid.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Blot Fresh / Rehydrate Dried + Dawn First
homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh: stack paper towels, step on them with shoe-weight, wick urine up. Dried / crusty: lay a COLD damp white towel on the spot 10 min to rehydrate, then blot. Then mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cold water. Dab onto the area with a white cloth, wait 5 minutes, blot. Dawn lifts surface fat + salts so the enzyme (next step) can reach the uric-acid crystals below. NO OxiClean — alkaline can set protein.

Apply a pet-specific enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle, Rocco & Roxie, Anti Icky Poo, Simple Solution). Saturate the area to PAD DEPTH — use 2–3× the volume of urine you estimate soaked in. The enzyme has to reach wherever the urine reached. This is the single most important step.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Wool carpet bans these — different card. Don't mix with steam, bleach, or strong alkali.

homeSolutions.agent: Cover and Dwell — Keep It Wet
homeSolutions.instruction: Lay a damp (not dripping) white towel over the treated area and cover with plastic wrap, tape edges. Keeps enzyme wet during dwell. Fresh: 30–60 min. Older / overnight: full 24 hours. The enzyme is digesting uric acid and needs time.
Uncover. Stack paper towels, weight with a heavy book, leave overnight to wick residual moisture out of pad. Fan-dry. Don't vacuum until fully dry.

Nose right down into the pile in daylight. Blacklight in a dark room. Smell gone + no fluorescence = win. Still positive = ONE more round of steps 5–7 with full 24-hour dwell. Still positive after TWO full rounds = urine is in the pad / subfloor — pro sub-surface extraction (NOT regular truck-mount cleaning).

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Sub-Surface Extraction for Pad Contamination
homeSolutions.instruction: Tell them: "Pet urine on [nylon / polyester / olefin] synthetic carpet, blacklight still positive after 2 enzyme rounds (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie). No heat, no ammonia. Possible pad or subfloor contamination — need sub-surface extraction tool, NOT truck-mount."

steps: 1. WOOL CARPET? Use pet-urine-wool-carpet — retail enzymes digest wool.
2. UV blacklight + tape full contaminated area; treat 4" beyond visible.
3. Two rules: NO hot water / steam / dryer. NO ammonia cleaners.
4. Cold blot fresh OR rehydrate dried with cold damp towel.
5. Dawn cold damp cloth, 5 min — clears surface for enzyme. NO OxiClean.
6. Pet enzyme — SATURATE to pad depth (2–3× urine volume).
7. Cover with damp towel + plastic wrap. Dwell 30 min (fresh) / 24 hr (older).
9. Re-blacklight + smell. 2 rounds max — then pro sub-surface extraction.
products: Pet enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution) — SYNTHETIC carpet only
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after 2 full enzyme rounds — pad / subfloor contamination needs pro sub-surface extraction. Wool carpet = use wool-specific card from minute one.
warnings: WOOL CARPET = different card (retail enzymes digest wool)
NEVER hot water / steam / dryer — sets protein, kills enzymes
NEVER OxiClean / alkaline before enzyme — can set protein
Over-wetting carries urine into pad — saturate enzyme, not water
2 home rounds max — then pro sub-surface extraction

neverDo: Never on wool carpet (retail pet enzymes digest wool)
Never hot water / steam / dryer on pet urine
Never OxiClean / alkaline before enzyme step
Never repeat home rounds beyond 2 — pad contamination needs pro
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic carpet (nylon / polyester / olefin / triexta): tolerates full enzyme protocol
Wool carpet: protease digests keratin — separate card (pet-urine-wool-carpet)
Heat sets protein permanently across all fibers

materialWarnings: Enzymes are the chemistry unlock for uric acid
Saturation to pad depth, not surface mist
2 rounds max — pad contamination = pro sub-surface extraction

escalation.when: Blacklight or smell positive after 2 full enzyme rounds, recurring same-spot accidents (pad almost certainly contaminated), wool carpet, or cat urine with persistent felinine mercaptan smell.
escalation.whatToTell: "Pet urine on [nylon / polyester / olefin] carpet, [cat / dog], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with enzyme ([brand]) × 2 rounds with 24-hr dwells. Blacklight still positive. No heat, no ammonia, no OxiClean. Possible pad or subfloor contamination — need sub-surface extraction tool, NOT truck-mount."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with sub-surface extraction tool (truck-mount alone is NOT sufficient for pad contamination)

contextQuestions: Is this WOOL carpet? (different card — retail enzymes digest wool)
Cat or dog? (cat = harder, felinine + mercaptans)
Has anyone applied ammonia / steam / OxiClean?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Cat urine with felinine + mercaptans is the hardest variant — full 24-hr enzyme cycle even for fresh. Recurring same-spot accidents = pad contamination almost certain — skip home rounds 3+ and go pro. Wool carpet = always pet-urine-wool-carpet card.
